title: Influencer vs Word of Mouth
description: Broadcast reach vs organic trust — which spreads more effectively and why marketing strategy depends on the answer.

influencer_reach.label: Influencer Reach
influencer_reach.description: How many people the influencer broadcasts to immediately

wom_trust.label: WoM Trust Factor
wom_trust.description: Trust multiplier for peer recommendations vs broadcast

narration.text: Gold large circle = influencer. Small green = word-of-mouth advocates. The influencer broadcasts to many instantly. WoM spreads slowly, peer to peer.
The influencer reaches many quickly — yellow 'heard' signals spread wide. But 'heard' doesn't mean 'converted'. Broadcast creates awareness, not trust.
Word-of-mouth is slower but converts more deeply. A recommendation from a friend triggers purchase behaviour at 4× the rate of advertising.
The influencer has plateaued — it reached everyone it could reach. WoM is still growing because each converted person becomes a new source.
For most products: influencers for initial awareness, WoM for conversion and loyalty. The mistake is paying for reach when you need trust.
Net Promoter Score measures this: how many customers become active word-of-mouth advocates? One advocate creates 3-5 customers over their lifetime.
